notice: GENERATED CODE - DO NOT MODIFY BY HAND.

feature.name: Event Planning
feature.description: Orchestrates the merged joined-and-saved Calendar agenda plus the saved-only route projection, their date/list recovery states, and Event Detail handoffs.

actionScope.included: Retrying identity, planned-event, and organizer-name loads; selecting a date; jumping to today; expanding or collapsing the calendar header; and opening one planned event.
excluded: Merging, deduplicating, sorting, status labeling, summary derivation, and organizer-name lookup are read-model projection behavior rather than user actions.
Automatic selected-day scrolling and scroll-driven header collapse are lifecycle consequences of date/header decisions, not separate actions.
Booking, cancellation, save, and unsave actions retain Event Detail ownership.

actions.description: Invalidates the current identity lookup after an authentication-provider failure.
Invalidates both joined-event and saved-event streams so the merged agenda reloads coherently.
Retries the secondary organizer-name lookup without discarding the already loaded event agenda.
Selects one visible date and reveals the corresponding agenda day when present.
Uses the persistent Today action to restore the reference date selection.
Expands or collapses the bounded week-strip versus month-grid header mode.
Opens Event Detail for one joined or saved agenda row.

evidenceExceptions.reason: These Calendar states have deterministic captures and Widgetbook evidence but no focused state-level test recorded by the screen authority.
Calendar event navigation is focused-test and Widgetbook proven, but the route authority does not register a dedicated post-selection capture.

actionScope.included: Closing the standalone route, retrying identity, saved-event, and organizer-name loads, and opening one saved event.
excluded: Saved-event ordering, past/saved status labeling, missing-document omission, and organizer-name projection are read-model behavior rather than user actions.
The route intentionally exposes no save or unsave mutation; those decisions retain Event Detail ownership.
System back gestures are the platform equivalent of the declared close action, not an additional feature action.

actions.description: Closes the standalone saved-events route through its canonical back control.
Invalidates the current identity lookup after an authentication-provider failure.
Invalidates the saved-event detail stream for the current viewer.
Retries organizer-name enrichment without discarding loaded saved-event rows.
Opens Event Detail for one saved-event row.

evidenceExceptions.reason: These Saved Events states have deterministic captures and Widgetbook evidence but no focused state-level test recorded by the screen authority.
Saved-event navigation is focused-test and Widgetbook proven, but the route authority does not register a dedicated post-selection capture.
